title: A lost wallet
description: For each question, write the correct answer. Write one word for each gap.

content.text: Yesterday, I found a wallet on the pavement while I was walking home from school. At first, I did not know what to do, but I decided to take it (27) the nearest police station. Inside the wallet, there was a student card, some money and a photograph. The police officer thanked me (28) bringing it in and said they would try to contact the owner. Later that evening, a boy from another school called to say the wallet was his. He had lost it while getting (29) the bus near the park. He sounded very relieved because the student card was important for his exams. The next day, he came to my house with his mother and thanked me again. I was pleased (30) I had done the right thing. My dad said it was better to be honest (31) keep something that was not mine. I hope someone would do the same for me (32) I lost something important.
